1	package agent
2	
3	// execute_bead_land.go — the land coordinator pattern.
4	//
5	// This file implements the human-PR landing model for execute-bead results.
6	// The old Merge() path in execute_bead_orchestrator.go has been deleted; all
7	// target-ref writes now flow through Land(), which is called exactly once per
8	// submission by a per-project coordinator goroutine (see
9	// internal/server/workers.go:LandCoordinator).
10	//
11	// The flow mirrors how a human merges PRs:
12	//
13	//   1. Fetch the current target tip (from origin when a remote exists, or
14	//      from the local branch otherwise).
15	//   2. If the current tip equals the worker's BaseRev — fast-forward the
16	//      target branch directly to the worker's ResultRev via update-ref. The
17	//      worker's commit keeps its original parent; no new commit is created.
18	//   3. Otherwise — the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
19	//      a temporary detached worktree at the current target tip, run
20	//      `git merge --no-ff` to introduce the worker's ResultRev, and
21	//      fast-forward the target branch to the resulting merge commit. The
22	//      worker's commit is NEVER rewritten: its parent is still BaseRev, so
23	//      a later replay observes the same inputs the worker saw.
24	//   4. If the merge conflicts — abort cleanly, preserve the original
25	//      ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>,
26	//      and return preserved status. Target branch is never modified.
27	//   5. If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip. The push is
28	//      strictly fast-forward; push failures are reported via PushFailed but
29	//      do not roll back the local target ref.
30	//
31	// Replay fidelity is the reason for merge-over-rebase: a rebased commit has
32	// a rewritten parent that lies about what the worker saw at execution time.
33	// A merge commit preserves both histories — the worker's original commit
34	// (parent = BaseRev) and the target's prior tip — losslessly.
35	//
36	// The in-process coordinator serializes submissions inside one process. Land()
37	// also takes the process-shared main-git lock so separate `ddx work` processes
38	// cannot interleave target-ref updates, evidence commits, or checkout sync.
39	
40	import (
41		"bytes"
42		"context"
43		"encoding/json"
44		"errors"
45		"fmt"
46		"go/parser"
47		"go/token"
48		"os"
49		"os/exec"
50		"path/filepath"
51		"strconv"
52		"strings"
53		"time"
54	
55		internalgit "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
56	)
57	
58	const defaultLargeDeletionLineThreshold = 200
59	
60	// LandRequest is one submission to the land coordinator: "here is the worker's
61	// result from base B to result R for bead X; land it on the project's target
62	// branch."
63	type LandRequest struct {
64		// WorktreeDir is the path to the project's repository directory (the
65		// directory the coordinator operates on). The original worker worktree
66		// has typically already been removed by the time Land() runs — Land()
67		// creates its own temporary worktrees when a merge is needed.
68		WorktreeDir string
69	
70		// BaseRev is the revision the worker branched off when it started.
71		// When the current target tip equals BaseRev, Land() takes the fast path.
72		BaseRev string
73	
74		// ResultRev is the worker's final commit SHA. Must be reachable in the
75		// project's git object store at the time Land() is called.
76		ResultRev string
77	
78		// BeadID identifies the bead this submission is for. Used to build the
79		// preserve-ref path on conflict.
80		BeadID string
81	
82		// AttemptID uniquely identifies this land attempt. Used to build the
83		// preserve-ref path on conflict so concurrent attempts for the same
84		// bead do not collide.
85		AttemptID string
86	
87		// TargetBranch is the branch to advance. When empty, Land() resolves
88		// the project's current HEAD branch and uses that.
89		TargetBranch string
90	
91		// EvidenceDir is the relative path to the per-attempt execution evidence
92		// directory (e.g. ".ddx/executions/20260416T181205-b5419982"). When
93		// non-empty and the main land succeeds, Land() creates a trailing
94		// evidence commit that folds these files into the target branch. The
95		// agent's commit SHA is NOT amended — the evidence commit is a separate
96		// child commit, preserving closing_commit_sha references.
97		EvidenceDir string
98	
99		// PostLandCommand is an optional project verification command run after
100		// Land() advances the local target ref and syncs the worktree, but before

100		// Land() advances the local target ref and syncs the worktree, but before
101		// evidence commit creation or push. A failure restores the target ref to
102		// its pre-land SHA and preserves ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations.
103		PostLandCommand []string
104	
105		// LargeDeletionLineThreshold overrides the default per-file deletion gate
106		// threshold. Zero or negative uses defaultLargeDeletionLineThreshold.
107		LargeDeletionLineThreshold int
108	}
109	
110	// LandResult describes the outcome of a single Land() call.
111	type LandResult struct {
112		// Status is one of:
113		//   - "landed":    the target branch now points at a new commit
114		//                  (either ResultRev itself on the fast-forward path,
115		//                  or a merge commit on the merge path).
116		//   - "preserved": the merge conflicted; ResultRev is saved under
117		//                  PreserveRef and the target branch is unchanged.
118		//   - "no-changes": ResultRev == BaseRev; nothing to land.
119		Status string
120	
121		// NewTip is the new value of the target branch when Status == "landed".
122		// On the fast-forward path NewTip == ResultRev; on the merge path NewTip
123		// is the SHA of the merge commit (whose parents are the prior target
124		// tip and ResultRev). Empty when preserved or no-changes.
125		NewTip string
126	
127		// Merged is true when the land took the merge-commit path (current tip
128		// had advanced beyond BaseRev, so Land() created a merge commit to
129		// combine the worker's result with the new target tip). False on the
130		// fast-forward path where the worker's commit became the new tip
131		// unchanged.
132		Merged bool
133	
134		// PreserveRef is set when Status == "preserved". It names the ref under
135		// refs/ddx/iterations/ where ResultRev was saved for later recovery.
136		PreserveRef string
137	
138		// Reason is a human-readable explanation, especially useful when
139		// Status == "preserved" (e.g. "merge conflict") or when PushFailed.
140		Reason string
141	
142		// PushFailed is true when the local target ref was advanced successfully
143		// but the subsequent push to origin was rejected (e.g. non-fast-forward).
144		// The local state is authoritative; the remote will need to be
145		// reconciled by a later land or an operator.
146		PushFailed bool
147	
148		// PushError captures the underlying push error when PushFailed is true.
149		PushError string
150	
151		// PushConflict is true when the initial push was rejected (e.g. origin
152		// advanced) and the loop's automatic pull/merge/retry recovery ran into
153		// a merge conflict it cannot resolve without operator input. Distinct
154		// from PushFailed so the loop can park the bead for human review under
155		// a short cooldown instead of treating it as a generic push failure.
156		PushConflict bool
157	
158		// PushRecovered is true when the initial push was rejected but the loop
159		// successfully fetched, merged, and re-pushed without operator input.
160		// The land is fully complete; PushFailed and PushConflict are false.
161		// Set so callers can record telemetry on how often the auto-recovery
162		// path actually saves an operator round-trip.
163		PushRecovered bool
164	
165		// MergedCommitCount is the number of commits reachable from ResultRev but
166		// not from BaseRev — i.e. the "size" of the worker's contribution being
167		// merged in. Zero on the no-changes path. Set on both the fast-forward
168		// and merge-commit paths so metrics can compare contribution sizes.
169		MergedCommitCount int
170	
171		// EvidenceCommitSHA is set when a trailing execution-evidence commit was
172		// created after the main land. When set, NewTip points at this commit
173		// (not at the original agent commit or merge commit).
174		EvidenceCommitSHA string
175	
176		// CheckoutSyncDeferred is true when the target ref advanced but DDx left
177		// the operator checkout files untouched because dirty local paths overlap
178		// files changed by the new target tip.
179		CheckoutSyncDeferred bool
180	
181		// CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths lists the dirty paths that caused checkout sync
182		// to be skipped.
183		CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths []string
184	}
185	
186	// PreClaimResult is the outcome of a FetchOriginAncestryCheck call.
187	type PreClaimResult struct {
188		// Action is one of:
189		//   "unchanged"     — local tip == origin tip, no action taken
190		//   "fast-forwarded"— local was behind origin; local branch was advanced
191		//   "no-origin"     — no origin remote; check skipped
192		//   "local-ahead"   — local is ahead of origin; no action needed
193		//   "diverged"      — neither is ancestor of the other; claim should be aborted
194		Action    string
195		LocalSHA  string
196		OriginSHA string
197	}
198	
199	type fetchOriginError struct {
200		targetBranch string
201		output       string
202		err          error
203	}
204	
205	func (e *fetchOriginError) Error() string {
206		if e.output == "" {
207			return fmt.Sprintf("git fetch origin %s: %v", e.targetBranch, e.err)
208		}
209		return fmt.Sprintf("git fetch origin %s: %s: %v", e.targetBranch, e.output, e.err)
210	}
211	
212	func (e *fetchOriginError) Unwrap() error { return e.err }
213	
214	// IsIgnorableFetchOriginError reports whether a pre-claim ancestry-check
215	// failure came from the best-effort network fetch. Local worktree safety
216	// failures must propagate so workers do not claim work from an unsafe trunk.
217	func IsIgnorableFetchOriginError(err error) bool {
218		if err == nil {
219			return false
220		}
221		var fetchErr *fetchOriginError
222		if errors.As(err, &fetchErr) {
223			return true
224		}
225		msg := err.Error()
226		return strings.HasPrefix(msg, "git fetch origin ") || strings.HasPrefix(msg, "git fetch origin:")
227	}
228	
229	// LandingGitOps abstracts the git operations Land() needs. RealLandingGitOps
230	// shells out to git; tests inject fakes or run against real temp repos.
231	type LandingGitOps interface {
232		// HasRemote reports whether the given remote name exists in dir.
233		HasRemote(dir, remote string) bool
234	
235		// CurrentBranch returns the branch HEAD currently points at in dir, or
236		// an error if HEAD is detached or unresolvable.
237		CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error)
238	
239		// FetchBranch fetches remote/branch into dir (no merge, no checkout).
240		// Returns nil when no remote exists.
241		FetchBranch(dir, remote, branch string) error
242	
243		// ResolveRef resolves ref (e.g. "refs/heads/main" or "origin/main") to a
244		// commit SHA. When ref is unresolvable returns ("", error).
245		ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error)
246	
247		// UpdateRefTo updates ref in dir to sha. When oldSHA is non-empty, the
248		// update is conditional on the current ref value equalling oldSHA.
249		UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error
250	
251		// SyncWorkTreeToHead syncs the index AND the working-tree files in dir
252		// to HEAD after a non-checkout ref update (e.g. update-ref). fromRev is
253		// the commit HEAD pointed at BEFORE the ref update; it is used to
254		// compute the minimal set of tracked files changed by the update so
255		// that unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat
256		// writes, operator scratch) are NOT clobbered.
257		//
258		// Needed because Land() advances the target ref via update-ref, which
259		// touches neither the index nor the worktree. Before this fix, Land()
260		// only ran `git read-tree HEAD` to sync the index — leaving the main
261		// worktree showing phantom deleted/modified entries for every file the
262		// landed commit touched, and subsequent agents reading files from disk
263		// would see stale content.
264		//
265		// Implementation: `git read-tree HEAD` + `git diff --name-only fromRev
266		// HEAD` to enumerate changed files + `git checkout-index -f --` to
267		// materialize them from the freshly-synced index.
268		SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error
269	
270		// AddWorktree creates a new worktree at path checked out at rev in dir.
271		AddWorktree(dir, path, rev string) error
272	
273		// AddBranchWorktree creates a new worktree at path checked out on branch
274		// in dir. It is used for clean landing finalization when the operator
275		// checkout already has the target branch checked out.
276		AddBranchWorktree(dir, path, branch string) error
277	
278		// RemoveWorktree removes the worktree at path in dir (force).
279		RemoveWorktree(dir, path string) error
280	
281		// MergeInto runs `git merge --no-ff -m msg srcRev` inside wtDir, which
282		// must already be checked out at the current target tip. A clean merge
283		// produces a merge commit whose parents are [currentTip, srcRev]; the
284		// commit SHA can be read with HeadRevAt. Returns nil on clean merge,
285		// or an error on conflict. On error, the implementation is responsible
286		// for running `git merge --abort` so the worktree is left clean.
287		MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error
288	
289		// HeadRevAt returns HEAD of the git workdir at dir.
290		HeadRevAt(dir string) (string, error)
291	
292		// PushFFOnly pushes localRef to remote as targetBranch with strict
293		// fast-forward semantics. Returns an error when the push would not be
294		// fast-forward or when the network call fails.
295		PushFFOnly(dir, remote, localRef, targetBranch string) error
296	
297		// FetchOriginAncestryCheck fetches origin/targetBranch and compares it
298		// to the local branch tip. When origin is ahead the local branch is
299		// fast-forwarded via update-ref + read-tree. When the two tips have
300		// diverged (neither is ancestor of the other) the returned result has
301		// Action == "diverged". When no origin remote exists the result has
302		// Action == "no-origin". The caller decides whether to abort a claim.
303		FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error)
304	
305		// CountCommits returns the number of commits reachable from tip but not
306		// from base (i.e. git rev-list --count base..tip). Used to record the
307		// contribution size in land metrics. Returns 0 on error.
308		CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int
309	
310		// StageDir stages all files under relPath in dir for the next commit.
311		StageDir(dir, relPath string) error
312	
313		// CommitStaged creates a commit from currently staged changes using msg
314		// as the commit message. Returns (sha, nil) when a commit was made,
315		// ("", nil) when nothing was staged, and ("", err) on failure.
316		CommitStaged(dir, msg string) (string, error)
317	
318		// DiffNumstat returns the output of `git diff --numstat base tip --` in
319		// dir. Used by the large-deletion gate. Returning ("", nil) indicates no
320		// diffable output (e.g. in a test stub with no real repo).
321		DiffNumstat(dir, base, tip string) (string, error)
322	
323		// DiffNameOnly returns the list of changed file paths between base and tip
324		// in dir (`git diff --name-only base tip --`). Used by the syntax-sanity
325		// gate. Returning (nil, nil) indicates no changes.
326		DiffNameOnly(dir, base, tip string) ([]string, error)
327	}
328	
329	// RealLandingGitOps implements LandingGitOps via os/exec git commands.
330	type RealLandingGitOps struct{}
331	
332	func (RealLandingGitOps) HasRemote(dir, remote string) bool {
333		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "remote").Output()
334		if err != nil {
335			return false
336		}
337		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), "\n") {
338			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == remote {
339				return true
340			}
341		}
342		return false
343	}
344	
345	func (RealLandingGitOps) CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error) {
346		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "symbolic-ref", "--short", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
347		if err != nil {
348			return "", fmt.Errorf("git symbolic-ref HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
349		}
350		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
351	}
352	
353	func (RealLandingGitOps) FetchBranch(dir, remote, branch string) error {
354		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "fetch", remote, branch).CombinedOutput()
355		if err != nil {
356			return fmt.Errorf("git fetch %s %s: %s: %w", remote, branch, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
357		}
358		return nil
359	}
360	
361	func (RealLandingGitOps) ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error) {
362		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref).CombinedOutput()
363		if err != nil {
364			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
365		}
366		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
367	}
368	
369	func (RealLandingGitOps) UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error {
370		if ref == "HEAD" {
371			return fmt.Errorf("refusing to update HEAD directly; landing must target a concrete ref")
372		}
373		args := []string{"update-ref", ref, sha}
374		if oldSHA != "" {
375			args = append(args, oldSHA)
376		}
377		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
378		if err != nil {
379			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
380		}
381		return nil
382	}
383	
384	// alwaysSkipSyncPaths lists live-state files governed by bead.Store WithLock.
385	// The committed worktree version is a stale snapshot of tracker state at claim
386	// time; the main-worktree copy is always authoritative and must never be
387	// overwritten by SyncWorkTreeToHead regardless of dirty-before-land state.
388	var alwaysSkipSyncPaths = []string{
389		".ddx/beads.jsonl",
390		".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl",
391	}
392	
393	func (RealLandingGitOps) SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error {
394		return syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths(dir, fromRev, alwaysSkipSyncPaths)
395	}
396	
397	// readTreeHeadWithRetry runs `git read-tree HEAD` in dir, retrying with
398	// exponential backoff when the git index lock is held by a concurrent process.
399	//

1	package agent
2	
3	// execute_bead_land_test.go — Land() coordinator-pattern unit tests.
4	//
5	// These tests run against a real temp git repo so they exercise the actual
6	// git plumbing (update-ref, merge --no-ff, worktree add, etc.) rather than a
7	// mock. Each scenario asserts that the target tip advances correctly and —
8	// crucially for replay fidelity — that the worker's own commit is never
9	// rewritten. Its parent always stays BaseRev so replay sees the same inputs
10	// the worker saw at execution time.
11	
12	import (
13		"fmt"
14		"os"
15		"os/exec"
16		"path/filepath"
17		"strings"
18		"sync"
19		"testing"
20		"time"
21	)
22	
23	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	// Test helpers (real git repo fixtures)
25	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	
27	type landTestRepo struct {
28		t       *testing.T
29		dir     string
30		origin  string // path to a bare origin repo, or "" if no remote
31		branch  string // target branch
32		baseSHA string // the initial commit on the target branch
33	}
34	
35	func newLandTestRepo(t *testing.T) *landTestRepo {
36		t.Helper()
37		dir := t.TempDir()
38		r := &landTestRepo{t: t, dir: dir, branch: "main"}
39		r.runGit("init", "-b", "main")
40		r.runGit("config", "user.name", "Test")
41		r.runGit("config", "user.email", "test@test.local")
42		r.writeFile("README.md", "# test\n")
43		r.runGit("add", "-A")
44		r.runGit("commit", "-m", "init")
45		r.baseSHA = r.resolveRef("refs/heads/main")
46		return r
47	}
48	
49	// newLandTestRepoWithOrigin creates a test repo whose origin is a separate
50	// bare repo. Used by the push-ff-only test.
51	func newLandTestRepoWithOrigin(t *testing.T) *landTestRepo {
52		t.Helper()
53		r := newLandTestRepo(t)
54	
55		bareDir := t.TempDir()
56		cmd := exec.Command("git", "init", "--bare", "-b", "main", bareDir)
57		if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
58			t.Fatalf("git init --bare: %s: %v", string(out), err)
59		}
60		r.origin = bareDir
61		r.runGit("remote", "add", "origin", bareDir)
62		r.runGit("push", "-u", "origin", "main")
63		return r
64	}
65	
66	func (r *landTestRepo) runGit(args ...string) string {
67		r.t.Helper()
68		cmd := exec.Command("git", append([]string{"-C", r.dir}, args...)...)
69		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
70		if err != nil {
71			r.t.Fatalf("git %s: %s: %v", strings.Join(args, " "), string(out), err)
72		}
73		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
